44/*
45 * If a catcache invalidation is processed while we are in the middle of
46 * creating a catcache entry (or list), it might apply to the entry we're
47 * creating, making it invalid before it's been inserted to the catcache.  To
48 * catch such cases, we have a stack of "create-in-progress" entries.  Cache
49 * invalidation marks any matching entries in the stack as dead, in addition
50 * to the actual CatCTup and CatCList entries.
51 */
52typedef struct CatCInProgress
53{
54	CatCache   *cache;			/* cache that the entry belongs to */
55	uint32		hash_value;		/* hash of the entry; ignored for lists */
56	bool		list;			/* is it a list entry? */
57	bool		dead;			/* set when the entry is invalidated */
58	struct CatCInProgress *next;
59} CatCInProgress;
60
61static CatCInProgress *catcache_in_progress_stack = NULL;
62
63 /* #define CACHEDEBUG */	/* turns DEBUG elogs on */
64
65/*
66 * Given a hash value and the size of the hash table, find the bucket
67 * in which the hash value belongs. Since the hash table must contain
68 * a power-of-2 number of elements, this is a simple bitmask.
69 */
70#define HASH_INDEX(h, sz) ((Index) ((h) & ((sz) - 1)))
71
72
73/*
74 *		variables, macros and other stuff
75 */
76
77#ifdef CACHEDEBUG
78#define CACHE_elog(...)				elog(__VA_ARGS__)
79#else
80#define CACHE_elog(...)
81#endif

137static const ResourceOwnerDesc catcache_resowner_desc =
138{
139	/* catcache references */
140	.name = "catcache reference",
141	.release_phase = RESOURCE_RELEASE_AFTER_LOCKS,
142	.release_priority = RELEASE_PRIO_CATCACHE_REFS,
143	.ReleaseResource = ResOwnerReleaseCatCache,
144	.DebugPrint = ResOwnerPrintCatCache
145};
146
147static const ResourceOwnerDesc catlistref_resowner_desc =
148{
149	/* catcache-list pins */
150	.name = "catcache list reference",
151	.release_phase = RESOURCE_RELEASE_AFTER_LOCKS,
152	.release_priority = RELEASE_PRIO_CATCACHE_LIST_REFS,
153	.ReleaseResource = ResOwnerReleaseCatCacheList,
154	.DebugPrint = ResOwnerPrintCatCacheList
155};

157/* Convenience wrappers over ResourceOwnerRemember/Forget */
158static inline void
159ResourceOwnerRememberCatCacheRef(ResourceOwner owner, HeapTuple tuple)
160{
161	ResourceOwnerRemember(owner, PointerGetDatum(tuple), &catcache_resowner_desc);
162}
163static inline void
164ResourceOwnerForgetCatCacheRef(ResourceOwner owner, HeapTuple tuple)
165{
166	ResourceOwnerForget(owner, PointerGetDatum(tuple), &catcache_resowner_desc);
167}
168static inline void
169ResourceOwnerRememberCatCacheListRef(ResourceOwner owner, CatCList *list)
170{
171	ResourceOwnerRemember(owner, PointerGetDatum(list), &catlistref_resowner_desc);
172}
173static inline void
174ResourceOwnerForgetCatCacheListRef(ResourceOwner owner, CatCList *list)
175{
176	ResourceOwnerForget(owner, PointerGetDatum(list), &catlistref_resowner_desc);
177}
178
179
180/*
181 * Hash and equality functions for system types that are used as cache key
182 * fields.  In some cases, we just call the regular SQL-callable functions for
183 * the appropriate data type, but that tends to be a little slow, and the
184 * speed of these functions is performance-critical.  Therefore, for data
185 * types that frequently occur as catcache keys, we hard-code the logic here.
186 * Avoiding the overhead of DirectFunctionCallN(...) is a substantial win, and
187 * in certain cases (like int4) we can adopt a faster hash algorithm as well.
188 */
189
190static bool
191chareqfast(Datum a, Datum b)
192{
193	return DatumGetChar(a) == DatumGetChar(b);
194}
195
196static uint32
197charhashfast(Datum datum)
198{
199	return murmurhash32((int32) DatumGetChar(datum));
200}
201
202static bool
203nameeqfast(Datum a, Datum b)
204{
205	char	   *ca = NameStr(*DatumGetName(a));
206	char	   *cb = NameStr(*DatumGetName(b));
207
208	/*
209	 * Catalogs only use deterministic collations, so ignore column collation
210	 * and use fast path.
211	 */
212	return strncmp(ca, cb, NAMEDATALEN) == 0;
213}
214
215static uint32
216namehashfast(Datum datum)
217{
218	char	   *key = NameStr(*DatumGetName(datum));
219
220	/*
221	 * Catalogs only use deterministic collations, so ignore column collation
222	 * and use fast path.
223	 */
224	return hash_any((unsigned char *) key, strlen(key));
225}
226
227static bool
228int2eqfast(Datum a, Datum b)
229{
230	return DatumGetInt16(a) == DatumGetInt16(b);
231}
232
233static uint32
234int2hashfast(Datum datum)
235{
236	return murmurhash32((int32) DatumGetInt16(datum));
237}
238
239static bool
240int4eqfast(Datum a, Datum b)
241{
242	return DatumGetInt32(a) == DatumGetInt32(b);
243}
244
245static uint32
246int4hashfast(Datum datum)
247{
248	return murmurhash32((int32) DatumGetInt32(datum));
249}
250
251static bool
252texteqfast(Datum a, Datum b)
253{
254	/*
255	 * Catalogs only use deterministic collations, so ignore column collation
256	 * and use "C" locale for efficiency.
257	 */
258	return DatumGetBool(DirectFunctionCall2Coll(texteq, C_COLLATION_OID, a, b));
259}
260
261static uint32
262texthashfast(Datum datum)
263{
264	/*
265	 * Catalogs only use deterministic collations, so ignore column collation
266	 * and use "C" locale for efficiency.
267	 */
268	return DatumGetInt32(DirectFunctionCall1Coll(hashtext, C_COLLATION_OID, datum));
269}
270
271static bool
272oidvectoreqfast(Datum a, Datum b)
273{
274	return DatumGetBool(DirectFunctionCall2(oidvectoreq, a, b));
275}
276
277static uint32
278oidvectorhashfast(Datum datum)
279{
280	return DatumGetInt32(DirectFunctionCall1(hashoidvector, datum));
281}
282
283/* Lookup support functions for a type. */
284static void
285GetCCHashEqFuncs(Oid keytype, CCHashFN *hashfunc, RegProcedure *eqfunc, CCFastEqualFN *fasteqfunc)
286{
287	switch (keytype)
288	{
289		case BOOLOID:
290			*hashfunc = charhashfast;
291			*fasteqfunc = chareqfast;
292			*eqfunc = F_BOOLEQ;
293			break;
294		case CHAROID:
295			*hashfunc = charhashfast;
296			*fasteqfunc = chareqfast;
297			*eqfunc = F_CHAREQ;
298			break;
299		case NAMEOID:
300			*hashfunc = namehashfast;
301			*fasteqfunc = nameeqfast;
302			*eqfunc = F_NAMEEQ;
303			break;
304		case INT2OID:
305			*hashfunc = int2hashfast;
306			*fasteqfunc = int2eqfast;
307			*eqfunc = F_INT2EQ;
308			break;
309		case INT4OID:
310			*hashfunc = int4hashfast;
311			*fasteqfunc = int4eqfast;
312			*eqfunc = F_INT4EQ;
313			break;
314		case TEXTOID:
315			*hashfunc = texthashfast;
316			*fasteqfunc = texteqfast;
317			*eqfunc = F_TEXTEQ;
318			break;
319		case OIDOID:
320		case REGPROCOID:
321		case REGPROCEDUREOID:
322		case REGOPEROID:
323		case REGOPERATOROID:
324		case REGCLASSOID:
325		case REGTYPEOID:
326		case REGCOLLATIONOID:
327		case REGCONFIGOID:
328		case REGDICTIONARYOID:
329		case REGROLEOID:
330		case REGNAMESPACEOID:
331			*hashfunc = int4hashfast;
332			*fasteqfunc = int4eqfast;
333			*eqfunc = F_OIDEQ;
334			break;
335		case OIDVECTOROID:
336			*hashfunc = oidvectorhashfast;
337			*fasteqfunc = oidvectoreqfast;
338			*eqfunc = F_OIDVECTOREQ;
339			break;
340		default:
341			elog(FATAL, "type %u not supported as catcache key", keytype);
342			*hashfunc = NULL;	/* keep compiler quiet */
343
344			*eqfunc = InvalidOid;
345			break;
346	}
347}
348
349/*
350 *		CatalogCacheComputeHashValue
351 *
352 * Compute the hash value associated with a given set of lookup keys
353 */
354static uint32
355CatalogCacheComputeHashValue(CatCache *cache, int nkeys,
356							 Datum v1, Datum v2, Datum v3, Datum v4)
357{
358	uint32		hashValue = 0;
359	uint32		oneHash;
360	CCHashFN   *cc_hashfunc = cache->cc_hashfunc;
361
362	CACHE_elog(DEBUG2, "CatalogCacheComputeHashValue %s %d %p",
363			   cache->cc_relname, nkeys, cache);
364
365	switch (nkeys)
366	{
367		case 4:
368			oneHash = (cc_hashfunc[3]) (v4);
369			hashValue ^= pg_rotate_left32(oneHash, 24);
370			/* FALLTHROUGH */
371		case 3:
372			oneHash = (cc_hashfunc[2]) (v3);
373			hashValue ^= pg_rotate_left32(oneHash, 16);
374			/* FALLTHROUGH */
375		case 2:
376			oneHash = (cc_hashfunc[1]) (v2);
377			hashValue ^= pg_rotate_left32(oneHash, 8);
378			/* FALLTHROUGH */
379		case 1:
380			oneHash = (cc_hashfunc[0]) (v1);
381			hashValue ^= oneHash;
382			break;
383		default:
384			elog(FATAL, "wrong number of hash keys: %d", nkeys);
385			break;
386	}
387
388	return hashValue;
389}
390
391/*
392 *		CatalogCacheComputeTupleHashValue
393 *
394 * Compute the hash value associated with a given tuple to be cached
395 */
396static uint32
397CatalogCacheComputeTupleHashValue(CatCache *cache, int nkeys, HeapTuple tuple)
398{
399	Datum		v1 = 0,
400				v2 = 0,
401				v3 = 0,
402				v4 = 0;
403	bool		isNull = false;
404	int		   *cc_keyno = cache->cc_keyno;
405	TupleDesc	cc_tupdesc = cache->cc_tupdesc;
406
407	/* Now extract key fields from tuple, insert into scankey */
408	switch (nkeys)
409	{
410		case 4:
411			v4 = fastgetattr(tuple,
412							 cc_keyno[3],
413							 cc_tupdesc,
414							 &isNull);
415			Assert(!isNull);
416			/* FALLTHROUGH */
417		case 3:
418			v3 = fastgetattr(tuple,
419							 cc_keyno[2],
420							 cc_tupdesc,
421							 &isNull);
422			Assert(!isNull);
423			/* FALLTHROUGH */
424		case 2:
425			v2 = fastgetattr(tuple,
426							 cc_keyno[1],
427							 cc_tupdesc,
428							 &isNull);
429			Assert(!isNull);
430			/* FALLTHROUGH */
431		case 1:
432			v1 = fastgetattr(tuple,
433							 cc_keyno[0],
434							 cc_tupdesc,
435							 &isNull);
436			Assert(!isNull);
437			break;
438		default:
439			elog(FATAL, "wrong number of hash keys: %d", nkeys);
440			break;
441	}
442
443	return CatalogCacheComputeHashValue(cache, nkeys, v1, v2, v3, v4);
444}
445
446/*
447 *		CatalogCacheCompareTuple
448 *
449 * Compare a tuple to the passed arguments.
450 */
451static inline bool
452CatalogCacheCompareTuple(const CatCache *cache, int nkeys,
453						 const Datum *cachekeys,
454						 const Datum *searchkeys)
455{
456	const CCFastEqualFN *cc_fastequal = cache->cc_fastequal;
457	int			i;
458
459	for (i = 0; i < nkeys; i++)
460	{
461		if (!(cc_fastequal[i]) (cachekeys[i], searchkeys[i]))
462			return false;
463	}
464	return true;
465}
466
467
468#ifdef CATCACHE_STATS
469
470static void
471CatCachePrintStats(int code, Datum arg)
472{
473	slist_iter	iter;
474	long		cc_searches = 0;
475	long		cc_hits = 0;
476	long		cc_neg_hits = 0;
477	long		cc_newloads = 0;
478	long		cc_invals = 0;
479	long		cc_nlists = 0;
480	long		cc_lsearches = 0;
481	long		cc_lhits = 0;
482
483	slist_foreach(iter, &CacheHdr->ch_caches)
484	{
485		CatCache   *cache = slist_container(CatCache, cc_next, iter.cur);
486
487		if (cache->cc_ntup == 0 && cache->cc_searches == 0)
488			continue;			/* don't print unused caches */
489		elog(DEBUG2, "catcache %s/%u: %d tup, %ld srch, %ld+%ld=%ld hits, %ld+%ld=%ld loads, %ld invals, %d lists, %ld lsrch, %ld lhits",
490			 cache->cc_relname,
491			 cache->cc_indexoid,
492			 cache->cc_ntup,
493			 cache->cc_searches,
494			 cache->cc_hits,
495			 cache->cc_neg_hits,
496			 cache->cc_hits + cache->cc_neg_hits,
497			 cache->cc_newloads,
498			 cache->cc_searches - cache->cc_hits - cache->cc_neg_hits - cache->cc_newloads,
499			 cache->cc_searches - cache->cc_hits - cache->cc_neg_hits,
500			 cache->cc_invals,
501			 cache->cc_nlist,
502			 cache->cc_lsearches,
503			 cache->cc_lhits);
504		cc_searches += cache->cc_searches;
505		cc_hits += cache->cc_hits;
506		cc_neg_hits += cache->cc_neg_hits;
507		cc_newloads += cache->cc_newloads;
508		cc_invals += cache->cc_invals;
509		cc_nlists += cache->cc_nlist;
510		cc_lsearches += cache->cc_lsearches;
511		cc_lhits += cache->cc_lhits;
512	}
513	elog(DEBUG2, "catcache totals: %d tup, %ld srch, %ld+%ld=%ld hits, %ld+%ld=%ld loads, %ld invals, %ld lists, %ld lsrch, %ld lhits",
514		 CacheHdr->ch_ntup,
515		 cc_searches,
516		 cc_hits,
517		 cc_neg_hits,
518		 cc_hits + cc_neg_hits,
519		 cc_newloads,
520		 cc_searches - cc_hits - cc_neg_hits - cc_newloads,
521		 cc_searches - cc_hits - cc_neg_hits,
522		 cc_invals,
523		 cc_nlists,
524		 cc_lsearches,
525		 cc_lhits);
526}
527#endif							/* CATCACHE_STATS */
528
529
530/*
531 *		CatCacheRemoveCTup
532 *
533 * Unlink and delete the given cache entry
534 *
535 * NB: if it is a member of a CatCList, the CatCList is deleted too.
536 * Both the cache entry and the list had better have zero refcount.
537 */
538static void
539CatCacheRemoveCTup(CatCache *cache, CatCTup *ct)
540{
541	Assert(ct->refcount == 0);
542	Assert(ct->my_cache == cache);
543
544	if (ct->c_list)
545	{
546		/*
547		 * The cleanest way to handle this is to call CatCacheRemoveCList,
548		 * which will recurse back to me, and the recursive call will do the
549		 * work.  Set the "dead" flag to make sure it does recurse.
550		 */
551		ct->dead = true;
552		CatCacheRemoveCList(cache, ct->c_list);
553		return;					/* nothing left to do */
554	}
555
556	/* delink from linked list */
557	dlist_delete(&ct->cache_elem);
558
559	/*
560	 * Free keys when we're dealing with a negative entry, normal entries just
561	 * point into tuple, allocated together with the CatCTup.
562	 */
563	if (ct->negative)
564		CatCacheFreeKeys(cache->cc_tupdesc, cache->cc_nkeys,
565						 cache->cc_keyno, ct->keys);
566
567	pfree(ct);
568
569	--cache->cc_ntup;
570	--CacheHdr->ch_ntup;
571}
572
573/*
574 *		CatCacheRemoveCList
575 *
576 * Unlink and delete the given cache list entry
577 *
578 * NB: any dead member entries that become unreferenced are deleted too.
579 */
580static void
581CatCacheRemoveCList(CatCache *cache, CatCList *cl)
582{
583	int			i;
584
585	Assert(cl->refcount == 0);
586	Assert(cl->my_cache == cache);
587
588	/* delink from member tuples */
589	for (i = cl->n_members; --i >= 0;)
590	{
591		CatCTup    *ct = cl->members[i];
592
593		Assert(ct->c_list == cl);
594		ct->c_list = NULL;
595		/* if the member is dead and now has no references, remove it */
596		if (
597#ifndef CATCACHE_FORCE_RELEASE
598			ct->dead &&
599#endif
600			ct->refcount == 0)
601			CatCacheRemoveCTup(cache, ct);
602	}
603
604	/* delink from linked list */
605	dlist_delete(&cl->cache_elem);
606
607	/* free associated column data */
608	CatCacheFreeKeys(cache->cc_tupdesc, cl->nkeys,
609					 cache->cc_keyno, cl->keys);
610
611	pfree(cl);
612
613	--cache->cc_nlist;
614}
615
616
617/*
618 *	CatCacheInvalidate
619 *
620 *	Invalidate entries in the specified cache, given a hash value.
621 *
622 *	We delete cache entries that match the hash value, whether positive
623 *	or negative.  We don't care whether the invalidation is the result
624 *	of a tuple insertion or a deletion.
625 *
626 *	We used to try to match positive cache entries by TID, but that is
627 *	unsafe after a VACUUM FULL on a system catalog: an inval event could
628 *	be queued before VACUUM FULL, and then processed afterwards, when the
629 *	target tuple that has to be invalidated has a different TID than it
630 *	did when the event was created.  So now we just compare hash values and
631 *	accept the small risk of unnecessary invalidations due to false matches.
632 *
633 *	This routine is only quasi-public: it should only be used by inval.c.
634 */
635void
636CatCacheInvalidate(CatCache *cache, uint32 hashValue)
637{
638	Index		hashIndex;
639	dlist_mutable_iter iter;
640
641	CACHE_elog(DEBUG2, "CatCacheInvalidate: called");
642
643	/*
644	 * We don't bother to check whether the cache has finished initialization
645	 * yet; if not, there will be no entries in it so no problem.
646	 */
647
648	/*
649	 * Invalidate *all* CatCLists in this cache; it's too hard to tell which
650	 * searches might still be correct, so just zap 'em all.
651	 */
652	for (int i = 0; i < cache->cc_nlbuckets; i++)
653	{
654		dlist_head *bucket = &cache->cc_lbucket[i];
655
656		dlist_foreach_modify(iter, bucket)
657		{
658			CatCList   *cl = dlist_container(CatCList, cache_elem, iter.cur);
659
660			if (cl->refcount > 0)
661				cl->dead = true;
662			else
663				CatCacheRemoveCList(cache, cl);
664		}
665	}
666
667	/*
668	 * inspect the proper hash bucket for tuple matches
669	 */
670	hashIndex = HASH_INDEX(hashValue, cache->cc_nbuckets);
671	dlist_foreach_modify(iter, &cache->cc_bucket[hashIndex])
672	{
673		CatCTup    *ct = dlist_container(CatCTup, cache_elem, iter.cur);
674
675		if (hashValue == ct->hash_value)
676		{
677			if (ct->refcount > 0 ||
678				(ct->c_list && ct->c_list->refcount > 0))
679			{
680				ct->dead = true;
681				/* list, if any, was marked dead above */
682				Assert(ct->c_list == NULL || ct->c_list->dead);
683			}
684			else
685				CatCacheRemoveCTup(cache, ct);
686			CACHE_elog(DEBUG2, "CatCacheInvalidate: invalidated");
687#ifdef CATCACHE_STATS
688			cache->cc_invals++;
689#endif
690			/* could be multiple matches, so keep looking! */
691		}
692	}
693
694	/* Also invalidate any entries that are being built */
695	for (CatCInProgress *e = catcache_in_progress_stack; e != NULL; e = e->next)
696	{
697		if (e->cache == cache)
698		{
699			if (e->list || e->hash_value == hashValue)
700				e->dead = true;
701		}
702	}
703}
704
705/* ----------------------------------------------------------------
706 *					   public functions
707 * ----------------------------------------------------------------
708 */
709
710
711/*
712 * Standard routine for creating cache context if it doesn't exist yet
713 *
714 * There are a lot of places (probably far more than necessary) that check
715 * whether CacheMemoryContext exists yet and want to create it if not.
716 * We centralize knowledge of exactly how to create it here.
717 */
718void
719CreateCacheMemoryContext(void)
720{
721	/*
722	 * Purely for paranoia, check that context doesn't exist; caller probably
723	 * did so already.
724	 */
725	if (!CacheMemoryContext)
726		CacheMemoryContext = AllocSetContextCreate(TopMemoryContext,
727												   "CacheMemoryContext",
728												   ALLOCSET_DEFAULT_SIZES);
729}
730
731
732/*
733 *		ResetCatalogCache
734 *
735 * Reset one catalog cache to empty.
736 *
737 * This is not very efficient if the target cache is nearly empty.
738 * However, it shouldn't need to be efficient; we don't invoke it often.
739 *
740 * If 'debug_discard' is true, we are being called as part of
741 * debug_discard_caches.  In that case, the cache is not reset for
742 * correctness, but just to get more testing of cache invalidation.  We skip
743 * resetting in-progress build entries in that case, or we'd never make any
744 * progress.
745 */
746static void
747ResetCatalogCache(CatCache *cache, bool debug_discard)
748{
749	dlist_mutable_iter iter;
750	int			i;
751
752	/* Remove each list in this cache, or at least mark it dead */
753	for (i = 0; i < cache->cc_nlbuckets; i++)
754	{
755		dlist_head *bucket = &cache->cc_lbucket[i];
756
757		dlist_foreach_modify(iter, bucket)
758		{
759			CatCList   *cl = dlist_container(CatCList, cache_elem, iter.cur);
760
761			if (cl->refcount > 0)
762				cl->dead = true;
763			else
764				CatCacheRemoveCList(cache, cl);
765		}
766	}
767
768	/* Remove each tuple in this cache, or at least mark it dead */
769	for (i = 0; i < cache->cc_nbuckets; i++)
770	{
771		dlist_head *bucket = &cache->cc_bucket[i];
772
773		dlist_foreach_modify(iter, bucket)
774		{
775			CatCTup    *ct = dlist_container(CatCTup, cache_elem, iter.cur);
776
777			if (ct->refcount > 0 ||
778				(ct->c_list && ct->c_list->refcount > 0))
779			{
780				ct->dead = true;
781				/* list, if any, was marked dead above */
782				Assert(ct->c_list == NULL || ct->c_list->dead);
783			}
784			else
785				CatCacheRemoveCTup(cache, ct);
786#ifdef CATCACHE_STATS
787			cache->cc_invals++;
788#endif
789		}
790	}
791
792	/* Also invalidate any entries that are being built */
793	if (!debug_discard)
794	{
795		for (CatCInProgress *e = catcache_in_progress_stack; e != NULL; e = e->next)
796		{
797			if (e->cache == cache)
798				e->dead = true;
799		}
800	}
801}
802
803/*
804 *		ResetCatalogCaches
805 *
806 * Reset all caches when a shared cache inval event forces it
807 */
808void
809ResetCatalogCaches(void)
810{
811	ResetCatalogCachesExt(false);
812}
813
814void
815ResetCatalogCachesExt(bool debug_discard)
816{
817	slist_iter	iter;
818
819	CACHE_elog(DEBUG2, "ResetCatalogCaches called");
820
821	slist_foreach(iter, &CacheHdr->ch_caches)
822	{
823		CatCache   *cache = slist_container(CatCache, cc_next, iter.cur);
824
825		ResetCatalogCache(cache, debug_discard);
826	}
827
828	CACHE_elog(DEBUG2, "end of ResetCatalogCaches call");
829}
830
831/*
832 *		CatalogCacheFlushCatalog
833 *
834 *	Flush all catcache entries that came from the specified system catalog.
835 *	This is needed after VACUUM FULL/CLUSTER on the catalog, since the
836 *	tuples very likely now have different TIDs than before.  (At one point
837 *	we also tried to force re-execution of CatalogCacheInitializeCache for
838 *	the cache(s) on that catalog.  This is a bad idea since it leads to all
839 *	kinds of trouble if a cache flush occurs while loading cache entries.
840 *	We now avoid the need to do it by copying cc_tupdesc out of the relcache,
841 *	rather than relying on the relcache to keep a tupdesc for us.  Of course
842 *	this assumes the tupdesc of a cachable system table will not change...)
843 */
844void
845CatalogCacheFlushCatalog(Oid catId)
846{
847	slist_iter	iter;
848
849	CACHE_elog(DEBUG2, "CatalogCacheFlushCatalog called for %u", catId);
850
851	slist_foreach(iter, &CacheHdr->ch_caches)
852	{
853		CatCache   *cache = slist_container(CatCache, cc_next, iter.cur);
854
855		/* Does this cache store tuples of the target catalog? */
856		if (cache->cc_reloid == catId)
857		{
858			/* Yes, so flush all its contents */
859			ResetCatalogCache(cache, false);
860
861			/* Tell inval.c to call syscache callbacks for this cache */
862			CallSyscacheCallbacks(cache->id, 0);
863		}
864	}
865
866	CACHE_elog(DEBUG2, "end of CatalogCacheFlushCatalog call");
867}
868
869/*
870 *		InitCatCache
871 *
872 *	This allocates and initializes a cache for a system catalog relation.
873 *	Actually, the cache is only partially initialized to avoid opening the
874 *	relation.  The relation will be opened and the rest of the cache
875 *	structure initialized on the first access.
876 */
877#ifdef CACHEDEBUG
878#define InitCatCache_DEBUG2 \
879do { \
880	elog(DEBUG2, "InitCatCache: rel=%u ind=%u id=%d nkeys=%d size=%d", \
881		 cp->cc_reloid, cp->cc_indexoid, cp->id, \
882		 cp->cc_nkeys, cp->cc_nbuckets); \
883} while(0)
884#else
885#define InitCatCache_DEBUG2
886#endif
887
888CatCache *
889InitCatCache(int id,
890			 Oid reloid,
891			 Oid indexoid,
892			 int nkeys,
893			 const int *key,
894			 int nbuckets)
895{
896	CatCache   *cp;
897	MemoryContext oldcxt;
898	int			i;
899
900	/*
901	 * nbuckets is the initial number of hash buckets to use in this catcache.
902	 * It will be enlarged later if it becomes too full.
903	 *
904	 * nbuckets must be a power of two.  We check this via Assert rather than
905	 * a full runtime check because the values will be coming from constant
906	 * tables.
907	 *
908	 * If you're confused by the power-of-two check, see comments in
909	 * bitmapset.c for an explanation.
910	 */
911	Assert(nbuckets > 0 && (nbuckets & -nbuckets) == nbuckets);
912
913	/*
914	 * first switch to the cache context so our allocations do not vanish at
915	 * the end of a transaction
916	 */
917	if (!CacheMemoryContext)
918		CreateCacheMemoryContext();
919
920	oldcxt = MemoryContextSwitchTo(CacheMemoryContext);
921
922	/*
923	 * if first time through, initialize the cache group header
924	 */
925	if (CacheHdr == NULL)
926	{
927		CacheHdr = (CatCacheHeader *) palloc(sizeof(CatCacheHeader));
928		slist_init(&CacheHdr->ch_caches);
929		CacheHdr->ch_ntup = 0;
930#ifdef CATCACHE_STATS
931		/* set up to dump stats at backend exit */
932		on_proc_exit(CatCachePrintStats, 0);
933#endif
934	}
935
936	/*
937	 * Allocate a new cache structure, aligning to a cacheline boundary
938	 *
939	 * Note: we rely on zeroing to initialize all the dlist headers correctly
940	 */
941	cp = (CatCache *) palloc_aligned(sizeof(CatCache), PG_CACHE_LINE_SIZE,
942									 MCXT_ALLOC_ZERO);
943	cp->cc_bucket = palloc0(nbuckets * sizeof(dlist_head));
944
945	/*
946	 * Many catcaches never receive any list searches.  Therefore, we don't
947	 * allocate the cc_lbuckets till we get a list search.
948	 */
949	cp->cc_lbucket = NULL;
950
951	/*
952	 * initialize the cache's relation information for the relation
953	 * corresponding to this cache, and initialize some of the new cache's
954	 * other internal fields.  But don't open the relation yet.
955	 */
956	cp->id = id;
957	cp->cc_relname = "(not known yet)";
958	cp->cc_reloid = reloid;
959	cp->cc_indexoid = indexoid;
960	cp->cc_relisshared = false; /* temporary */
961	cp->cc_tupdesc = (TupleDesc) NULL;
962	cp->cc_ntup = 0;
963	cp->cc_nlist = 0;
964	cp->cc_nbuckets = nbuckets;
965	cp->cc_nlbuckets = 0;
966	cp->cc_nkeys = nkeys;
967	for (i = 0; i < nkeys; ++i)
968	{
969		Assert(AttributeNumberIsValid(key[i]));
970		cp->cc_keyno[i] = key[i];
971	}
972
973	/*
974	 * new cache is initialized as far as we can go for now. print some
975	 * debugging information, if appropriate.
976	 */
977	InitCatCache_DEBUG2;
978
979	/*
980	 * add completed cache to top of group header's list
981	 */
982	slist_push_head(&CacheHdr->ch_caches, &cp->cc_next);
983
984	/*
985	 * back to the old context before we return...
986	 */
987	MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldcxt);
988
989	return cp;
990}
991
992/*
993 * Enlarge a catcache, doubling the number of buckets.
994 */
995static void
996RehashCatCache(CatCache *cp)
997{
998	dlist_head *newbucket;
999	int			newnbuckets;
1000	int			i;
1001
1002	elog(DEBUG1, "rehashing catalog cache id %d for %s; %d tups, %d buckets",
1003		 cp->id, cp->cc_relname, cp->cc_ntup, cp->cc_nbuckets);
1004
1005	/* Allocate a new, larger, hash table. */
1006	newnbuckets = cp->cc_nbuckets * 2;
1007	newbucket = (dlist_head *) MemoryContextAllocZero(CacheMemoryContext, newnbuckets * sizeof(dlist_head));
1008
1009	/* Move all entries from old hash table to new. */
1010	for (i = 0; i < cp->cc_nbuckets; i++)
1011	{
1012		dlist_mutable_iter iter;
1013
1014		dlist_foreach_modify(iter, &cp->cc_bucket[i])
1015		{
1016			CatCTup    *ct = dlist_container(CatCTup, cache_elem, iter.cur);
1017			int			hashIndex = HASH_INDEX(ct->hash_value, newnbuckets);
1018
1019			dlist_delete(iter.cur);
1020			dlist_push_head(&newbucket[hashIndex], &ct->cache_elem);
1021		}
1022	}
1023
1024	/* Switch to the new array. */
1025	pfree(cp->cc_bucket);
1026	cp->cc_nbuckets = newnbuckets;
1027	cp->cc_bucket = newbucket;
1028}
1029
1030/*
1031 * Enlarge a catcache's list storage, doubling the number of buckets.
1032 */
1033static void
1034RehashCatCacheLists(CatCache *cp)
1035{
1036	dlist_head *newbucket;
1037	int			newnbuckets;
1038	int			i;
1039
1040	elog(DEBUG1, "rehashing catalog cache id %d for %s; %d lists, %d buckets",
1041		 cp->id, cp->cc_relname, cp->cc_nlist, cp->cc_nlbuckets);
1042
1043	/* Allocate a new, larger, hash table. */
1044	newnbuckets = cp->cc_nlbuckets * 2;
1045	newbucket = (dlist_head *) MemoryContextAllocZero(CacheMemoryContext, newnbuckets * sizeof(dlist_head));
1046
1047	/* Move all entries from old hash table to new. */
1048	for (i = 0; i < cp->cc_nlbuckets; i++)
1049	{
1050		dlist_mutable_iter iter;
1051
1052		dlist_foreach_modify(iter, &cp->cc_lbucket[i])
1053		{
1054			CatCList   *cl = dlist_container(CatCList, cache_elem, iter.cur);
1055			int			hashIndex = HASH_INDEX(cl->hash_value, newnbuckets);
1056
1057			dlist_delete(iter.cur);
1058			dlist_push_head(&newbucket[hashIndex], &cl->cache_elem);
1059		}
1060	}
1061
1062	/* Switch to the new array. */
1063	pfree(cp->cc_lbucket);
1064	cp->cc_nlbuckets = newnbuckets;
1065	cp->cc_lbucket = newbucket;
1066}
1067
1068/*
1069 *		ConditionalCatalogCacheInitializeCache
1070 *
1071 * Call CatalogCacheInitializeCache() if not yet done.
1072 */
1073pg_attribute_always_inline
1074static void
1075ConditionalCatalogCacheInitializeCache(CatCache *cache)
1076{
1077#ifdef USE_ASSERT_CHECKING
1078	/*
1079	 * TypeCacheRelCallback() runs outside transactions and relies on TYPEOID
1080	 * for hashing.  This isn't ideal.  Since lookup_type_cache() both
1081	 * registers the callback and searches TYPEOID, reaching trouble likely
1082	 * requires OOM at an unlucky moment.
1083	 *
1084	 * InvalidateAttoptCacheCallback() runs outside transactions and likewise
1085	 * relies on ATTNUM.  InitPostgres() initializes ATTNUM, so it's reliable.
1086	 */
1087	if (!(cache->id == TYPEOID || cache->id == ATTNUM) ||
1088		IsTransactionState())
1089		AssertCouldGetRelation();
1090	else
1091		Assert(cache->cc_tupdesc != NULL);
1092#endif
1093
1094	if (unlikely(cache->cc_tupdesc == NULL))
1095		CatalogCacheInitializeCache(cache);
1096}
1097
1098/*
1099 *		CatalogCacheInitializeCache
1100 *
1101 * This function does final initialization of a catcache: obtain the tuple
1102 * descriptor and set up the hash and equality function links.
1103 */
1104#ifdef CACHEDEBUG
1105#define CatalogCacheInitializeCache_DEBUG1 \
1106	elog(DEBUG2, "CatalogCacheInitializeCache: cache @%p rel=%u", cache, \
1107		 cache->cc_reloid)
1108
1109#define CatalogCacheInitializeCache_DEBUG2 \
1110do { \
1111		if (cache->cc_keyno[i] > 0) { \
1112			elog(DEBUG2, "CatalogCacheInitializeCache: load %d/%d w/%d, %u", \
1113				i+1, cache->cc_nkeys, cache->cc_keyno[i], \
1114				 TupleDescAttr(tupdesc, cache->cc_keyno[i] - 1)->atttypid); \
1115		} else { \
1116			elog(DEBUG2, "CatalogCacheInitializeCache: load %d/%d w/%d", \
1117				i+1, cache->cc_nkeys, cache->cc_keyno[i]); \
1118		} \
1119} while(0)
1120#else
1121#define CatalogCacheInitializeCache_DEBUG1
1122#define CatalogCacheInitializeCache_DEBUG2
1123#endif
1124
1125static void
1126CatalogCacheInitializeCache(CatCache *cache)
1127{
1128	Relation	relation;
1129	MemoryContext oldcxt;
1130	TupleDesc	tupdesc;
1131	int			i;
1132
1133	CatalogCacheInitializeCache_DEBUG1;
1134
1135	relation = table_open(cache->cc_reloid, AccessShareLock);
1136
1137	/*
1138	 * switch to the cache context so our allocations do not vanish at the end
1139	 * of a transaction
1140	 */
1141	Assert(CacheMemoryContext != NULL);
1142
1143	oldcxt = MemoryContextSwitchTo(CacheMemoryContext);
1144
1145	/*
1146	 * copy the relcache's tuple descriptor to permanent cache storage
1147	 */
1148	tupdesc = CreateTupleDescCopyConstr(RelationGetDescr(relation));
1149
1150	/*
1151	 * save the relation's name and relisshared flag, too (cc_relname is used
1152	 * only for debugging purposes)
1153	 */
1154	cache->cc_relname = pstrdup(RelationGetRelationName(relation));
1155	cache->cc_relisshared = RelationGetForm(relation)->relisshared;
1156
1157	/*
1158	 * return to the caller's memory context and close the rel
1159	 */
1160	MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldcxt);
1161
1162	table_close(relation, AccessShareLock);
1163
1164	CACHE_elog(DEBUG2, "CatalogCacheInitializeCache: %s, %d keys",
1165			   cache->cc_relname, cache->cc_nkeys);
1166
1167	/*
1168	 * initialize cache's key information
1169	 */
1170	for (i = 0; i < cache->cc_nkeys; ++i)
1171	{
1172		Oid			keytype;
1173		RegProcedure eqfunc;
1174
1175		CatalogCacheInitializeCache_DEBUG2;
1176
1177		if (cache->cc_keyno[i] > 0)
1178		{
1179			Form_pg_attribute attr = TupleDescAttr(tupdesc,
1180												   cache->cc_keyno[i] - 1);
1181
1182			keytype = attr->atttypid;
1183			/* cache key columns should always be NOT NULL */
1184			Assert(attr->attnotnull);
1185		}
1186		else
1187		{
1188			if (cache->cc_keyno[i] < 0)
1189				elog(FATAL, "sys attributes are not supported in caches");
1190			keytype = OIDOID;
1191		}
1192
1193		GetCCHashEqFuncs(keytype,
1194						 &cache->cc_hashfunc[i],
1195						 &eqfunc,
1196						 &cache->cc_fastequal[i]);
1197
1198		/*
1199		 * Do equality-function lookup (we assume this won't need a catalog
1200		 * lookup for any supported type)
1201		 */
1202		fmgr_info_cxt(eqfunc,
1203					  &cache->cc_skey[i].sk_func,
1204					  CacheMemoryContext);
1205
1206		/* Initialize sk_attno suitably for HeapKeyTest() and heap scans */
1207		cache->cc_skey[i].sk_attno = cache->cc_keyno[i];
1208
1209		/* Fill in sk_strategy as well --- always standard equality */
1210		cache->cc_skey[i].sk_strategy = BTEqualStrategyNumber;
1211		cache->cc_skey[i].sk_subtype = InvalidOid;
1212		/* If a catcache key requires a collation, it must be C collation */
1213		cache->cc_skey[i].sk_collation = C_COLLATION_OID;
1214
1215		CACHE_elog(DEBUG2, "CatalogCacheInitializeCache %s %d %p",
1216				   cache->cc_relname, i, cache);
1217	}
1218
1219	/*
1220	 * mark this cache fully initialized
1221	 */
1222	cache->cc_tupdesc = tupdesc;
1223}
1224
1225/*
1226 * InitCatCachePhase2 -- external interface for CatalogCacheInitializeCache
1227 *
1228 * One reason to call this routine is to ensure that the relcache has
1229 * created entries for all the catalogs and indexes referenced by catcaches.
1230 * Therefore, provide an option to open the index as well as fixing the
1231 * cache itself.  An exception is the indexes on pg_am, which we don't use
1232 * (cf. IndexScanOK).
1233 */
1234void
1235InitCatCachePhase2(CatCache *cache, bool touch_index)
1236{
1237	ConditionalCatalogCacheInitializeCache(cache);
1238
1239	if (touch_index &&
1240		cache->id != AMOID &&
1241		cache->id != AMNAME)
1242	{
1243		Relation	idesc;
1244
1245		/*
1246		 * We must lock the underlying catalog before opening the index to
1247		 * avoid deadlock, since index_open could possibly result in reading
1248		 * this same catalog, and if anyone else is exclusive-locking this
1249		 * catalog and index they'll be doing it in that order.
1250		 */
1251		LockRelationOid(cache->cc_reloid, AccessShareLock);
1252		idesc = index_open(cache->cc_indexoid, AccessShareLock);
1253
1254		/*
1255		 * While we've got the index open, let's check that it's unique (and
1256		 * not just deferrable-unique, thank you very much).  This is just to
1257		 * catch thinkos in definitions of new catcaches, so we don't worry
1258		 * about the pg_am indexes not getting tested.
1259		 */
1260		Assert(idesc->rd_index->indisunique &&
1261			   idesc->rd_index->indimmediate);
1262
1263		index_close(idesc, AccessShareLock);
1264		UnlockRelationOid(cache->cc_reloid, AccessShareLock);
1265	}
1266}
1267
1268
1269/*
1270 *		IndexScanOK
1271 *
1272 *		This function checks for tuples that will be fetched by
1273 *		IndexSupportInitialize() during relcache initialization for
1274 *		certain system indexes that support critical syscaches.
1275 *		We can't use an indexscan to fetch these, else we'll get into
1276 *		infinite recursion.  A plain heap scan will work, however.
1277 *		Once we have completed relcache initialization (signaled by
1278 *		criticalRelcachesBuilt), we don't have to worry anymore.
1279 *
1280 *		Similarly, during backend startup we have to be able to use the
1281 *		pg_authid, pg_auth_members and pg_database syscaches for
1282 *		authentication even if we don't yet have relcache entries for those
1283 *		catalogs' indexes.
1284 */
1285static bool
1286IndexScanOK(CatCache *cache)
1287{
1288	switch (cache->id)
1289	{
1290		case INDEXRELID:
1291
1292			/*
1293			 * Rather than tracking exactly which indexes have to be loaded
1294			 * before we can use indexscans (which changes from time to time),
1295			 * just force all pg_index searches to be heap scans until we've
1296			 * built the critical relcaches.
1297			 */
1298			if (!criticalRelcachesBuilt)
1299				return false;
1300			break;
1301
1302		case AMOID:
1303		case AMNAME:
1304
1305			/*
1306			 * Always do heap scans in pg_am, because it's so small there's
1307			 * not much point in an indexscan anyway.  We *must* do this when
1308			 * initially building critical relcache entries, but we might as
1309			 * well just always do it.
1310			 */
1311			return false;
1312
1313		case AUTHNAME:
1314		case AUTHOID:
1315		case AUTHMEMMEMROLE:
1316		case DATABASEOID:
1317
1318			/*
1319			 * Protect authentication lookups occurring before relcache has
1320			 * collected entries for shared indexes.
1321			 */
1322			if (!criticalSharedRelcachesBuilt)
1323				return false;
1324			break;
1325
1326		default:
1327			break;
1328	}
1329
1330	/* Normal case, allow index scan */
1331	return true;
1332}
1333
1334/*
1335 *	SearchCatCache
1336 *
1337 *		This call searches a system cache for a tuple, opening the relation
1338 *		if necessary (on the first access to a particular cache).
1339 *
1340 *		The result is NULL if not found, or a pointer to a HeapTuple in
1341 *		the cache.  The caller must not modify the tuple, and must call
1342 *		ReleaseCatCache() when done with it.
1343 *
1344 * The search key values should be expressed as Datums of the key columns'
1345 * datatype(s).  (Pass zeroes for any unused parameters.)  As a special
1346 * exception, the passed-in key for a NAME column can be just a C string;
1347 * the caller need not go to the trouble of converting it to a fully
1348 * null-padded NAME.
1349 */
1350HeapTuple
1351SearchCatCache(CatCache *cache,
1352			   Datum v1,
1353			   Datum v2,
1354			   Datum v3,
1355			   Datum v4)
1356{
1357	return SearchCatCacheInternal(cache, cache->cc_nkeys, v1, v2, v3, v4);
1358}
1359
1360
1361/*
1362 * SearchCatCacheN() are SearchCatCache() versions for a specific number of
1363 * arguments. The compiler can inline the body and unroll loops, making them a
1364 * bit faster than SearchCatCache().
1365 */
1366
1367HeapTuple
1368SearchCatCache1(CatCache *cache,
1369				Datum v1)
1370{
1371	return SearchCatCacheInternal(cache, 1, v1, 0, 0, 0);
1372}
1373
1374
1375HeapTuple
1376SearchCatCache2(CatCache *cache,
1377				Datum v1, Datum v2)
1378{
1379	return SearchCatCacheInternal(cache, 2, v1, v2, 0, 0);
1380}
1381
1382
1383HeapTuple
1384SearchCatCache3(CatCache *cache,
1385				Datum v1, Datum v2, Datum v3)
1386{
1387	return SearchCatCacheInternal(cache, 3, v1, v2, v3, 0);
1388}
1389
1390
1391HeapTuple
1392SearchCatCache4(CatCache *cache,
1393				Datum v1, Datum v2, Datum v3, Datum v4)
1394{
1395	return SearchCatCacheInternal(cache, 4, v1, v2, v3, v4);
1396}
1397
1398/*
1399 * Work-horse for SearchCatCache/SearchCatCacheN.
1400 */
1401static inline HeapTuple
1402SearchCatCacheInternal(CatCache *cache,
1403					   int nkeys,
1404					   Datum v1,
1405					   Datum v2,
1406					   Datum v3,
1407					   Datum v4)
1408{
1409	Datum		arguments[CATCACHE_MAXKEYS];
1410	uint32		hashValue;
1411	Index		hashIndex;
1412	dlist_iter	iter;
1413	dlist_head *bucket;
1414	CatCTup    *ct;
1415
1416	Assert(cache->cc_nkeys == nkeys);
1417
1418	/*
1419	 * one-time startup overhead for each cache
1420	 */
1421	ConditionalCatalogCacheInitializeCache(cache);
1422
1423#ifdef CATCACHE_STATS
1424	cache->cc_searches++;
1425#endif
1426
1427	/* Initialize local parameter array */
1428	arguments[0] = v1;
1429	arguments[1] = v2;
1430	arguments[2] = v3;
1431	arguments[3] = v4;
1432
1433	/*
1434	 * find the hash bucket in which to look for the tuple
1435	 */
1436	hashValue = CatalogCacheComputeHashValue(cache, nkeys, v1, v2, v3, v4);
1437	hashIndex = HASH_INDEX(hashValue, cache->cc_nbuckets);
1438
1439	/*
1440	 * scan the hash bucket until we find a match or exhaust our tuples
1441	 *
1442	 * Note: it's okay to use dlist_foreach here, even though we modify the
1443	 * dlist within the loop, because we don't continue the loop afterwards.
1444	 */
1445	bucket = &cache->cc_bucket[hashIndex];
1446	dlist_foreach(iter, bucket)
1447	{
1448		ct = dlist_container(CatCTup, cache_elem, iter.cur);
1449
1450		if (ct->dead)
1451			continue;			/* ignore dead entries */
1452
1453		if (ct->hash_value != hashValue)
1454			continue;			/* quickly skip entry if wrong hash val */
1455
1456		if (!CatalogCacheCompareTuple(cache, nkeys, ct->keys, arguments))
1457			continue;
1458
1459		/*
1460		 * We found a match in the cache.  Move it to the front of the list
1461		 * for its hashbucket, in order to speed subsequent searches.  (The
1462		 * most frequently accessed elements in any hashbucket will tend to be
1463		 * near the front of the hashbucket's list.)
1464		 */
1465		dlist_move_head(bucket, &ct->cache_elem);
1466
1467		/*
1468		 * If it's a positive entry, bump its refcount and return it. If it's
1469		 * negative, we can report failure to the caller.
1470		 */
1471		if (!ct->negative)
1472		{
1473			ResourceOwnerEnlarge(CurrentResourceOwner);
1474			ct->refcount++;
1475			ResourceOwnerRememberCatCacheRef(CurrentResourceOwner, &ct->tuple);
1476
1477			CACHE_elog(DEBUG2, "SearchCatCache(%s): found in bucket %d",
1478					   cache->cc_relname, hashIndex);
1479
1480#ifdef CATCACHE_STATS
1481			cache->cc_hits++;
1482#endif
1483
1484			return &ct->tuple;
1485		}
1486		else
1487		{
1488			CACHE_elog(DEBUG2, "SearchCatCache(%s): found neg entry in bucket %d",
1489					   cache->cc_relname, hashIndex);
1490
1491#ifdef CATCACHE_STATS
1492			cache->cc_neg_hits++;
1493#endif
1494
1495			return NULL;
1496		}
1497	}
1498
1499	return SearchCatCacheMiss(cache, nkeys, hashValue, hashIndex, v1, v2, v3, v4);
1500}
1501
1502/*
1503 * Search the actual catalogs, rather than the cache.
1504 *
1505 * This is kept separate from SearchCatCacheInternal() to keep the fast-path
1506 * as small as possible.  To avoid that effort being undone by a helpful
1507 * compiler, try to explicitly forbid inlining.
1508 */
1509static pg_noinline HeapTuple
1510SearchCatCacheMiss(CatCache *cache,
1511				   int nkeys,
1512				   uint32 hashValue,
1513				   Index hashIndex,
1514				   Datum v1,
1515				   Datum v2,
1516				   Datum v3,
1517				   Datum v4)
1518{
1519	ScanKeyData cur_skey[CATCACHE_MAXKEYS];
1520	Relation	relation;
1521	SysScanDesc scandesc;
1522	HeapTuple	ntp;
1523	CatCTup    *ct;
1524	bool		stale;
1525	Datum		arguments[CATCACHE_MAXKEYS];
1526
1527	/* Initialize local parameter array */
1528	arguments[0] = v1;
1529	arguments[1] = v2;
1530	arguments[2] = v3;
1531	arguments[3] = v4;
1532
1533	/*
1534	 * Tuple was not found in cache, so we have to try to retrieve it directly
1535	 * from the relation.  If found, we will add it to the cache; if not
1536	 * found, we will add a negative cache entry instead.
1537	 *
1538	 * NOTE: it is possible for recursive cache lookups to occur while reading
1539	 * the relation --- for example, due to shared-cache-inval messages being
1540	 * processed during table_open().  This is OK.  It's even possible for one
1541	 * of those lookups to find and enter the very same tuple we are trying to
1542	 * fetch here.  If that happens, we will enter a second copy of the tuple
1543	 * into the cache.  The first copy will never be referenced again, and
1544	 * will eventually age out of the cache, so there's no functional problem.
1545	 * This case is rare enough that it's not worth expending extra cycles to
1546	 * detect.
1547	 *
1548	 * Another case, which we *must* handle, is that the tuple could become
1549	 * outdated during CatalogCacheCreateEntry's attempt to detoast it (since
1550	 * AcceptInvalidationMessages can run during TOAST table access).  We do
1551	 * not want to return already-stale catcache entries, so we loop around
1552	 * and do the table scan again if that happens.
1553	 */
1554	relation = table_open(cache->cc_reloid, AccessShareLock);
1555
1556	/*
1557	 * Ok, need to make a lookup in the relation, copy the scankey and fill
1558	 * out any per-call fields.
1559	 */
1560	memcpy(cur_skey, cache->cc_skey, sizeof(ScanKeyData) * nkeys);
1561	cur_skey[0].sk_argument = v1;
1562	cur_skey[1].sk_argument = v2;
1563	cur_skey[2].sk_argument = v3;
1564	cur_skey[3].sk_argument = v4;
1565
1566	do
1567	{
1568		scandesc = systable_beginscan(relation,
1569									  cache->cc_indexoid,
1570									  IndexScanOK(cache),
1571									  NULL,
1572									  nkeys,
1573									  cur_skey);
1574
1575		ct = NULL;
1576		stale = false;
1577
1578		while (HeapTupleIsValid(ntp = systable_getnext(scandesc)))
1579		{
1580			ct = CatalogCacheCreateEntry(cache, ntp, NULL,
1581										 hashValue, hashIndex);
1582			/* upon failure, we must start the scan over */
1583			if (ct == NULL)
1584			{
1585				stale = true;
1586				break;
1587			}
1588			/* immediately set the refcount to 1 */
1589			ResourceOwnerEnlarge(CurrentResourceOwner);
1590			ct->refcount++;
1591			ResourceOwnerRememberCatCacheRef(CurrentResourceOwner, &ct->tuple);
1592			break;				/* assume only one match */
1593		}
1594
1595		systable_endscan(scandesc);
1596	} while (stale);
1597
1598	table_close(relation, AccessShareLock);
1599
1600	/*
1601	 * If tuple was not found, we need to build a negative cache entry
1602	 * containing a fake tuple.  The fake tuple has the correct key columns,
1603	 * but nulls everywhere else.
1604	 *
1605	 * In bootstrap mode, we don't build negative entries, because the cache
1606	 * invalidation mechanism isn't alive and can't clear them if the tuple
1607	 * gets created later.  (Bootstrap doesn't do UPDATEs, so it doesn't need
1608	 * cache inval for that.)
1609	 */
1610	if (ct == NULL)
1611	{
1612		if (IsBootstrapProcessingMode())
1613			return NULL;
1614
1615		ct = CatalogCacheCreateEntry(cache, NULL, arguments,
1616									 hashValue, hashIndex);
1617
1618		/* Creating a negative cache entry shouldn't fail */
1619		Assert(ct != NULL);
1620
1621		CACHE_elog(DEBUG2, "SearchCatCache(%s): Contains %d/%d tuples",
1622				   cache->cc_relname, cache->cc_ntup, CacheHdr->ch_ntup);
1623		CACHE_elog(DEBUG2, "SearchCatCache(%s): put neg entry in bucket %d",
1624				   cache->cc_relname, hashIndex);
1625
1626		/*
1627		 * We are not returning the negative entry to the caller, so leave its
1628		 * refcount zero.
1629		 */
1630
1631		return NULL;
1632	}
1633
1634	CACHE_elog(DEBUG2, "SearchCatCache(%s): Contains %d/%d tuples",
1635			   cache->cc_relname, cache->cc_ntup, CacheHdr->ch_ntup);
1636	CACHE_elog(DEBUG2, "SearchCatCache(%s): put in bucket %d",
1637			   cache->cc_relname, hashIndex);
1638
1639#ifdef CATCACHE_STATS
1640	cache->cc_newloads++;
1641#endif
1642
1643	return &ct->tuple;
1644}
1645
1646/*
1647 *	ReleaseCatCache
1648 *
1649 *	Decrement the reference count of a catcache entry (releasing the
1650 *	hold grabbed by a successful SearchCatCache).
1651 *
1652 *	NOTE: if compiled with -DCATCACHE_FORCE_RELEASE then catcache entries
1653 *	will be freed as soon as their refcount goes to zero.  In combination
1654 *	with aset.c's CLOBBER_FREED_MEMORY option, this provides a good test
1655 *	to catch references to already-released catcache entries.
1656 */
1657void
1658ReleaseCatCache(HeapTuple tuple)
1659{
1660	ReleaseCatCacheWithOwner(tuple, CurrentResourceOwner);
1661}
1662
1663static void
1664ReleaseCatCacheWithOwner(HeapTuple tuple, ResourceOwner resowner)
1665{
1666	CatCTup    *ct = (CatCTup *) (((char *) tuple) -
1667								  offsetof(CatCTup, tuple));
1668
1669	/* Safety checks to ensure we were handed a cache entry */
1670	Assert(ct->ct_magic == CT_MAGIC);
1671	Assert(ct->refcount > 0);
1672
1673	ct->refcount--;
1674	if (resowner)
1675		ResourceOwnerForgetCatCacheRef(resowner, &ct->tuple);
1676
1677	if (
1678#ifndef CATCACHE_FORCE_RELEASE
1679		ct->dead &&
1680#endif
1681		ct->refcount == 0 &&
1682		(ct->c_list == NULL || ct->c_list->refcount == 0))
1683		CatCacheRemoveCTup(ct->my_cache, ct);
1684}
1685
1686
1687/*
1688 *	GetCatCacheHashValue
1689 *
1690 *		Compute the hash value for a given set of search keys.
1691 *
1692 * The reason for exposing this as part of the API is that the hash value is
1693 * exposed in cache invalidation operations, so there are places outside the
1694 * catcache code that need to be able to compute the hash values.
1695 */
1696uint32
1697GetCatCacheHashValue(CatCache *cache,
1698					 Datum v1,
1699					 Datum v2,
1700					 Datum v3,
1701					 Datum v4)
1702{
1703	/*
1704	 * one-time startup overhead for each cache
1705	 */
1706	ConditionalCatalogCacheInitializeCache(cache);
1707
1708	/*
1709	 * calculate the hash value
1710	 */
1711	return CatalogCacheComputeHashValue(cache, cache->cc_nkeys, v1, v2, v3, v4);
1712}
1713
1714
1715/*
1716 *	SearchCatCacheList
1717 *
1718 *		Generate a list of all tuples matching a partial key (that is,
1719 *		a key specifying just the first K of the cache's N key columns).
1720 *
1721 *		It doesn't make any sense to specify all of the cache's key columns
1722 *		here: since the key is unique, there could be at most one match, so
1723 *		you ought to use SearchCatCache() instead.  Hence this function takes
1724 *		one fewer Datum argument than SearchCatCache() does.
1725 *
1726 *		The caller must not modify the list object or the pointed-to tuples,
1727 *		and must call ReleaseCatCacheList() when done with the list.
1728 */
1729CatCList *
1730SearchCatCacheList(CatCache *cache,
1731				   int nkeys,
1732				   Datum v1,
1733				   Datum v2,
1734				   Datum v3)
1735{
1736	Datum		v4 = 0;			/* dummy last-column value */
1737	Datum		arguments[CATCACHE_MAXKEYS];
1738	uint32		lHashValue;
1739	Index		lHashIndex;
1740	dlist_iter	iter;
1741	dlist_head *lbucket;
1742	CatCList   *cl;
1743	CatCTup    *ct;
1744	List	   *volatile ctlist;
1745	ListCell   *ctlist_item;
1746	int			nmembers;
1747	bool		ordered;
1748	HeapTuple	ntp;
1749	MemoryContext oldcxt;
1750	int			i;
1751	CatCInProgress *save_in_progress;
1752	CatCInProgress in_progress_ent;
1753
1754	/*
1755	 * one-time startup overhead for each cache
1756	 */
1757	ConditionalCatalogCacheInitializeCache(cache);
1758
1759	Assert(nkeys > 0 && nkeys < cache->cc_nkeys);
1760
1761#ifdef CATCACHE_STATS
1762	cache->cc_lsearches++;
1763#endif
1764
1765	/* Initialize local parameter array */
1766	arguments[0] = v1;
1767	arguments[1] = v2;
1768	arguments[2] = v3;
1769	arguments[3] = v4;
1770
1771	/*
1772	 * If we haven't previously done a list search in this cache, create the
1773	 * bucket header array; otherwise, consider whether it's time to enlarge
1774	 * it.
1775	 */
1776	if (cache->cc_lbucket == NULL)
1777	{
1778		/* Arbitrary initial size --- must be a power of 2 */
1779		int			nbuckets = 16;
1780
1781		cache->cc_lbucket = (dlist_head *)
1782			MemoryContextAllocZero(CacheMemoryContext,
1783								   nbuckets * sizeof(dlist_head));
1784		/* Don't set cc_nlbuckets if we get OOM allocating cc_lbucket */
1785		cache->cc_nlbuckets = nbuckets;
1786	}
1787	else
1788	{
1789		/*
1790		 * If the hash table has become too full, enlarge the buckets array.
1791		 * Quite arbitrarily, we enlarge when fill factor > 2.
1792		 */
1793		if (cache->cc_nlist > cache->cc_nlbuckets * 2)
1794			RehashCatCacheLists(cache);
1795	}
1796
1797	/*
1798	 * Find the hash bucket in which to look for the CatCList.
1799	 */
1800	lHashValue = CatalogCacheComputeHashValue(cache, nkeys, v1, v2, v3, v4);
1801	lHashIndex = HASH_INDEX(lHashValue, cache->cc_nlbuckets);
1802
1803	/*
1804	 * scan the items until we find a match or exhaust our list
1805	 *
1806	 * Note: it's okay to use dlist_foreach here, even though we modify the
1807	 * dlist within the loop, because we don't continue the loop afterwards.
1808	 */
1809	lbucket = &cache->cc_lbucket[lHashIndex];
1810	dlist_foreach(iter, lbucket)
1811	{
1812		cl = dlist_container(CatCList, cache_elem, iter.cur);
1813
1814		if (cl->dead)
1815			continue;			/* ignore dead entries */
1816
1817		if (cl->hash_value != lHashValue)
1818			continue;			/* quickly skip entry if wrong hash val */
1819
1820		/*
1821		 * see if the cached list matches our key.
1822		 */
1823		if (cl->nkeys != nkeys)
1824			continue;
1825
1826		if (!CatalogCacheCompareTuple(cache, nkeys, cl->keys, arguments))
1827			continue;
1828
1829		/*
1830		 * We found a matching list.  Move the list to the front of the list
1831		 * for its hashbucket, so as to speed subsequent searches.  (We do not
1832		 * move the members to the fronts of their hashbucket lists, however,
1833		 * since there's no point in that unless they are searched for
1834		 * individually.)
1835		 */
1836		dlist_move_head(lbucket, &cl->cache_elem);
1837
1838		/* Bump the list's refcount and return it */
1839		ResourceOwnerEnlarge(CurrentResourceOwner);
1840		cl->refcount++;
1841		ResourceOwnerRememberCatCacheListRef(CurrentResourceOwner, cl);
1842
1843		CACHE_elog(DEBUG2, "SearchCatCacheList(%s): found list",
1844				   cache->cc_relname);
1845
1846#ifdef CATCACHE_STATS
1847		cache->cc_lhits++;
1848#endif
1849
1850		return cl;
1851	}
1852
1853	/*
1854	 * List was not found in cache, so we have to build it by reading the
1855	 * relation.  For each matching tuple found in the relation, use an
1856	 * existing cache entry if possible, else build a new one.
1857	 *
1858	 * We have to bump the member refcounts temporarily to ensure they won't
1859	 * get dropped from the cache while loading other members. We use a PG_TRY
1860	 * block to ensure we can undo those refcounts if we get an error before
1861	 * we finish constructing the CatCList.  ctlist must be valid throughout
1862	 * the PG_TRY block.
1863	 */
1864	ctlist = NIL;
1865
1866	/*
1867	 * Cache invalidation can happen while we're building the list.
1868	 * CatalogCacheCreateEntry() handles concurrent invalidation of individual
1869	 * tuples, but it's also possible that a new entry is concurrently added
1870	 * that should be part of the list we're building.  Register an
1871	 * "in-progress" entry that will receive the invalidation, until we have
1872	 * built the final list entry.
1873	 */
1874	save_in_progress = catcache_in_progress_stack;
1875	in_progress_ent.next = catcache_in_progress_stack;
1876	in_progress_ent.cache = cache;
1877	in_progress_ent.hash_value = lHashValue;
1878	in_progress_ent.list = true;
1879	in_progress_ent.dead = false;
1880	catcache_in_progress_stack = &in_progress_ent;
1881
1882	PG_TRY();
1883	{
1884		ScanKeyData cur_skey[CATCACHE_MAXKEYS];
1885		Relation	relation;
1886		SysScanDesc scandesc;
1887		bool		first_iter = true;
1888
1889		relation = table_open(cache->cc_reloid, AccessShareLock);
1890
1891		/*
1892		 * Ok, need to make a lookup in the relation, copy the scankey and
1893		 * fill out any per-call fields.
1894		 */
1895		memcpy(cur_skey, cache->cc_skey, sizeof(ScanKeyData) * cache->cc_nkeys);
1896		cur_skey[0].sk_argument = v1;
1897		cur_skey[1].sk_argument = v2;
1898		cur_skey[2].sk_argument = v3;
1899		cur_skey[3].sk_argument = v4;
1900
1901		/*
1902		 * Scan the table for matching entries.  If an invalidation arrives
1903		 * mid-build, we will loop back here to retry.
1904		 */
1905		do
1906		{
1907			/*
1908			 * If we are retrying, release refcounts on any items created on
1909			 * the previous iteration.  We dare not try to free them if
1910			 * they're now unreferenced, since an error while doing that would
1911			 * result in the PG_CATCH below doing extra refcount decrements.
1912			 * Besides, we'll likely re-adopt those items in the next
1913			 * iteration, so it's not worth complicating matters to try to get
1914			 * rid of them.
1915			 */
1916			foreach(ctlist_item, ctlist)
1917			{
1918				ct = (CatCTup *) lfirst(ctlist_item);
1919				Assert(ct->c_list == NULL);
1920				Assert(ct->refcount > 0);
1921				ct->refcount--;
1922			}
1923			/* Reset ctlist in preparation for new try */
1924			ctlist = NIL;
1925			in_progress_ent.dead = false;
1926
1927			scandesc = systable_beginscan(relation,
1928										  cache->cc_indexoid,
1929										  IndexScanOK(cache),
1930										  NULL,
1931										  nkeys,
1932										  cur_skey);
1933
1934			/* The list will be ordered iff we are doing an index scan */
1935			ordered = (scandesc->irel != NULL);
1936
1937			/* Injection point to help testing the recursive invalidation case */
1938			if (first_iter)
1939			{
1940				INJECTION_POINT("catcache-list-miss-systable-scan-started", NULL);
1941				first_iter = false;
1942			}
1943
1944			while (HeapTupleIsValid(ntp = systable_getnext(scandesc)) &&
1945				   !in_progress_ent.dead)
1946			{
1947				uint32		hashValue;
1948				Index		hashIndex;
1949				bool		found = false;
1950				dlist_head *bucket;
1951
1952				/*
1953				 * See if there's an entry for this tuple already.
1954				 */
1955				ct = NULL;
1956				hashValue = CatalogCacheComputeTupleHashValue(cache, cache->cc_nkeys, ntp);
1957				hashIndex = HASH_INDEX(hashValue, cache->cc_nbuckets);
1958
1959				bucket = &cache->cc_bucket[hashIndex];
1960				dlist_foreach(iter, bucket)
1961				{
1962					ct = dlist_container(CatCTup, cache_elem, iter.cur);
1963
1964					if (ct->dead || ct->negative)
1965						continue;	/* ignore dead and negative entries */
1966
1967					if (ct->hash_value != hashValue)
1968						continue;	/* quickly skip entry if wrong hash val */
1969
1970					if (!ItemPointerEquals(&(ct->tuple.t_self), &(ntp->t_self)))
1971						continue;	/* not same tuple */
1972
1973					/*
1974					 * Found a match, but can't use it if it belongs to
1975					 * another list already
1976					 */
1977					if (ct->c_list)
1978						continue;
1979
1980					found = true;
1981					break;		/* A-OK */
1982				}
1983
1984				if (!found)
1985				{
1986					/* We didn't find a usable entry, so make a new one */
1987					ct = CatalogCacheCreateEntry(cache, ntp, NULL,
1988												 hashValue, hashIndex);
1989
1990					/* upon failure, we must start the scan over */
1991					if (ct == NULL)
1992					{
1993						in_progress_ent.dead = true;
1994						break;
1995					}
1996				}
1997
1998				/* Careful here: add entry to ctlist, then bump its refcount */
1999				/* This way leaves state correct if lappend runs out of memory */
2000				ctlist = lappend(ctlist, ct);
2001				ct->refcount++;
2002			}
2003
2004			systable_endscan(scandesc);
2005		} while (in_progress_ent.dead);
2006
2007		table_close(relation, AccessShareLock);
2008
2009		/* Make sure the resource owner has room to remember this entry. */
2010		ResourceOwnerEnlarge(CurrentResourceOwner);
2011
2012		/* Now we can build the CatCList entry. */
2013		oldcxt = MemoryContextSwitchTo(CacheMemoryContext);
2014		nmembers = list_length(ctlist);
2015		cl = (CatCList *)
2016			palloc(offsetof(CatCList, members) + nmembers * sizeof(CatCTup *));
2017
2018		/* Extract key values */
2019		CatCacheCopyKeys(cache->cc_tupdesc, nkeys, cache->cc_keyno,
2020						 arguments, cl->keys);
2021		MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldcxt);
2022
2023		/*
2024		 * We are now past the last thing that could trigger an elog before we
2025		 * have finished building the CatCList and remembering it in the
2026		 * resource owner.  So it's OK to fall out of the PG_TRY, and indeed
2027		 * we'd better do so before we start marking the members as belonging
2028		 * to the list.
2029		 */
2030	}
2031	PG_CATCH();
2032	{
2033		Assert(catcache_in_progress_stack == &in_progress_ent);
2034		catcache_in_progress_stack = save_in_progress;
2035
2036		foreach(ctlist_item, ctlist)
2037		{
2038			ct = (CatCTup *) lfirst(ctlist_item);
2039			Assert(ct->c_list == NULL);
2040			Assert(ct->refcount > 0);
2041			ct->refcount--;
2042			if (
2043#ifndef CATCACHE_FORCE_RELEASE
2044				ct->dead &&
2045#endif
2046				ct->refcount == 0 &&
2047				(ct->c_list == NULL || ct->c_list->refcount == 0))
2048				CatCacheRemoveCTup(cache, ct);
2049		}
2050
2051		PG_RE_THROW();
2052	}
2053	PG_END_TRY();
2054	Assert(catcache_in_progress_stack == &in_progress_ent);
2055	catcache_in_progress_stack = save_in_progress;
2056
2057	cl->cl_magic = CL_MAGIC;
2058	cl->my_cache = cache;
2059	cl->refcount = 0;			/* for the moment */
2060	cl->dead = false;
2061	cl->ordered = ordered;
2062	cl->nkeys = nkeys;
2063	cl->hash_value = lHashValue;
2064	cl->n_members = nmembers;
2065
2066	i = 0;
2067	foreach(ctlist_item, ctlist)
2068	{
2069		cl->members[i++] = ct = (CatCTup *) lfirst(ctlist_item);
2070		Assert(ct->c_list == NULL);
2071		ct->c_list = cl;
2072		/* release the temporary refcount on the member */
2073		Assert(ct->refcount > 0);
2074		ct->refcount--;
2075		/* mark list dead if any members already dead */
2076		if (ct->dead)
2077			cl->dead = true;
2078	}
2079	Assert(i == nmembers);
2080
2081	/*
2082	 * Add the CatCList to the appropriate bucket, and count it.
2083	 */
2084	dlist_push_head(lbucket, &cl->cache_elem);
2085
2086	cache->cc_nlist++;
2087
2088	/* Finally, bump the list's refcount and return it */
2089	cl->refcount++;
2090	ResourceOwnerRememberCatCacheListRef(CurrentResourceOwner, cl);
2091
2092	CACHE_elog(DEBUG2, "SearchCatCacheList(%s): made list of %d members",
2093			   cache->cc_relname, nmembers);
2094
2095	return cl;
2096}
2097
2098/*
2099 *	ReleaseCatCacheList
2100 *
2101 *	Decrement the reference count of a catcache list.
2102 */
2103void
2104ReleaseCatCacheList(CatCList *list)
2105{
2106	ReleaseCatCacheListWithOwner(list, CurrentResourceOwner);
2107}
2108
2109static void
2110ReleaseCatCacheListWithOwner(CatCList *list, ResourceOwner resowner)
2111{
2112	/* Safety checks to ensure we were handed a cache entry */
2113	Assert(list->cl_magic == CL_MAGIC);
2114	Assert(list->refcount > 0);
2115	list->refcount--;
2116	if (resowner)
2117		ResourceOwnerForgetCatCacheListRef(resowner, list);
2118
2119	if (
2120#ifndef CATCACHE_FORCE_RELEASE
2121		list->dead &&
2122#endif
2123		list->refcount == 0)
2124		CatCacheRemoveCList(list->my_cache, list);
2125}
2126
2127
2128/*
2129 * CatalogCacheCreateEntry
2130 *		Create a new CatCTup entry, copying the given HeapTuple and other
2131 *		supplied data into it.  The new entry initially has refcount 0.
2132 *
2133 * To create a normal cache entry, ntp must be the HeapTuple just fetched
2134 * from scandesc, and "arguments" is not used.  To create a negative cache
2135 * entry, pass NULL for ntp; then "arguments" is the cache keys to use.
2136 * In either case, hashValue/hashIndex are the hash values computed from
2137 * the cache keys.
2138 *
2139 * Returns NULL if we attempt to detoast the tuple and observe that it
2140 * became stale.  (This cannot happen for a negative entry.)  Caller must
2141 * retry the tuple lookup in that case.
2142 */
2143static CatCTup *
2144CatalogCacheCreateEntry(CatCache *cache, HeapTuple ntp, Datum *arguments,
2145						uint32 hashValue, Index hashIndex)
2146{
2147	CatCTup    *ct;
2148	MemoryContext oldcxt;
2149
2150	if (ntp)
2151	{
2152		int			i;
2153		HeapTuple	dtp = NULL;
2154
2155		/*
2156		 * The invalidation of the in-progress entry essentially never happens
2157		 * during our regression tests, and there's no easy way to force it to
2158		 * fail for testing purposes.  To ensure we have test coverage for the
2159		 * retry paths in our callers, make debug builds randomly fail about
2160		 * 0.1% of the times through this code path, even when there's no
2161		 * toasted fields.
2162		 */
2163#ifdef USE_ASSERT_CHECKING
2164		if (pg_prng_uint32(&pg_global_prng_state) <= (PG_UINT32_MAX / 1000))
2165			return NULL;
2166#endif
2167
2168		/*
2169		 * If there are any out-of-line toasted fields in the tuple, expand
2170		 * them in-line.  This saves cycles during later use of the catcache
2171		 * entry, and also protects us against the possibility of the toast
2172		 * tuples being freed before we attempt to fetch them, in case of
2173		 * something using a slightly stale catcache entry.
2174		 */
2175		if (HeapTupleHasExternal(ntp))
2176		{
2177			CatCInProgress *save_in_progress;
2178			CatCInProgress in_progress_ent;
2179
2180			/*
2181			 * The tuple could become stale while we are doing toast table
2182			 * access (since AcceptInvalidationMessages can run then).  The
2183			 * invalidation will mark our in-progress entry as dead.
2184			 */
2185			save_in_progress = catcache_in_progress_stack;
2186			in_progress_ent.next = catcache_in_progress_stack;
2187			in_progress_ent.cache = cache;
2188			in_progress_ent.hash_value = hashValue;
2189			in_progress_ent.list = false;
2190			in_progress_ent.dead = false;
2191			catcache_in_progress_stack = &in_progress_ent;
2192
2193			PG_TRY();
2194			{
2195				dtp = toast_flatten_tuple(ntp, cache->cc_tupdesc);
2196			}
2197			PG_FINALLY();
2198			{
2199				Assert(catcache_in_progress_stack == &in_progress_ent);
2200				catcache_in_progress_stack = save_in_progress;
2201			}
2202			PG_END_TRY();
2203
2204			if (in_progress_ent.dead)
2205			{
2206				heap_freetuple(dtp);
2207				return NULL;
2208			}
2209		}
2210		else
2211			dtp = ntp;
2212
2213		/* Allocate memory for CatCTup and the cached tuple in one go */
2214		oldcxt = MemoryContextSwitchTo(CacheMemoryContext);
2215
2216		ct = (CatCTup *) palloc(sizeof(CatCTup) +
2217								MAXIMUM_ALIGNOF + dtp->t_len);
2218		ct->tuple.t_len = dtp->t_len;
2219		ct->tuple.t_self = dtp->t_self;
2220		ct->tuple.t_tableOid = dtp->t_tableOid;
2221		ct->tuple.t_data = (HeapTupleHeader)
2222			MAXALIGN(((char *) ct) + sizeof(CatCTup));
2223		/* copy tuple contents */
2224		memcpy((char *) ct->tuple.t_data,
2225			   (const char *) dtp->t_data,
2226			   dtp->t_len);
2227		MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldcxt);
2228
2229		if (dtp != ntp)
2230			heap_freetuple(dtp);
2231
2232		/* extract keys - they'll point into the tuple if not by-value */
2233		for (i = 0; i < cache->cc_nkeys; i++)
2234		{
2235			Datum		atp;
2236			bool		isnull;
2237
2238			atp = heap_getattr(&ct->tuple,
2239							   cache->cc_keyno[i],
2240							   cache->cc_tupdesc,
2241							   &isnull);
2242			Assert(!isnull);
2243			ct->keys[i] = atp;
2244		}
2245	}
2246	else
2247	{
2248		/* Set up keys for a negative cache entry */
2249		oldcxt = MemoryContextSwitchTo(CacheMemoryContext);
2250		ct = (CatCTup *) palloc(sizeof(CatCTup));
2251
2252		/*
2253		 * Store keys - they'll point into separately allocated memory if not
2254		 * by-value.
2255		 */
2256		CatCacheCopyKeys(cache->cc_tupdesc, cache->cc_nkeys, cache->cc_keyno,
2257						 arguments, ct->keys);
2258		MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldcxt);
2259	}
2260
2261	/*
2262	 * Finish initializing the CatCTup header, and add it to the cache's
2263	 * linked list and counts.
2264	 */
2265	ct->ct_magic = CT_MAGIC;
2266	ct->my_cache = cache;
2267	ct->c_list = NULL;
2268	ct->refcount = 0;			/* for the moment */
2269	ct->dead = false;
2270	ct->negative = (ntp == NULL);
2271	ct->hash_value = hashValue;
2272
2273	dlist_push_head(&cache->cc_bucket[hashIndex], &ct->cache_elem);
2274
2275	cache->cc_ntup++;
2276	CacheHdr->ch_ntup++;
2277
2278	/*
2279	 * If the hash table has become too full, enlarge the buckets array. Quite
2280	 * arbitrarily, we enlarge when fill factor > 2.
2281	 */
2282	if (cache->cc_ntup > cache->cc_nbuckets * 2)
2283		RehashCatCache(cache);
2284
2285	return ct;
2286}
2287
2288/*
2289 * Helper routine that frees keys stored in the keys array.
2290 */
2291static void
2292CatCacheFreeKeys(TupleDesc tupdesc, int nkeys, int *attnos, Datum *keys)
2293{
2294	int			i;
2295
2296	for (i = 0; i < nkeys; i++)
2297	{
2298		int			attnum = attnos[i];
2299		Form_pg_attribute att;
2300
2301		/* system attribute are not supported in caches */
2302		Assert(attnum > 0);
2303
2304		att = TupleDescAttr(tupdesc, attnum - 1);
2305
2306		if (!att->attbyval)
2307			pfree(DatumGetPointer(keys[i]));
2308	}
2309}
2310
2311/*
2312 * Helper routine that copies the keys in the srckeys array into the dstkeys
2313 * one, guaranteeing that the datums are fully allocated in the current memory
2314 * context.
2315 */
2316static void
2317CatCacheCopyKeys(TupleDesc tupdesc, int nkeys, int *attnos,
2318				 Datum *srckeys, Datum *dstkeys)
2319{
2320	int			i;
2321
2322	/*
2323	 * XXX: memory and lookup performance could possibly be improved by
2324	 * storing all keys in one allocation.
2325	 */
2326
2327	for (i = 0; i < nkeys; i++)
2328	{
2329		int			attnum = attnos[i];
2330		Form_pg_attribute att = TupleDescAttr(tupdesc, attnum - 1);
2331		Datum		src = srckeys[i];
2332		NameData	srcname;
2333
2334		/*
2335		 * Must be careful in case the caller passed a C string where a NAME
2336		 * is wanted: convert the given argument to a correctly padded NAME.
2337		 * Otherwise the memcpy() done by datumCopy() could fall off the end
2338		 * of memory.
2339		 */
2340		if (att->atttypid == NAMEOID)
2341		{
2342			namestrcpy(&srcname, DatumGetCString(src));
2343			src = NameGetDatum(&srcname);
2344		}
2345
2346		dstkeys[i] = datumCopy(src,
2347							   att->attbyval,
2348							   att->attlen);
2349	}
2350}
2351
2352/*
2353 *	PrepareToInvalidateCacheTuple()
2354 *
2355 *	This is part of a rather subtle chain of events, so pay attention:
2356 *
2357 *	When a tuple is inserted or deleted, it cannot be flushed from the
2358 *	catcaches immediately, for reasons explained at the top of cache/inval.c.
2359 *	Instead we have to add entry(s) for the tuple to a list of pending tuple
2360 *	invalidations that will be done at the end of the command or transaction.
2361 *
2362 *	The lists of tuples that need to be flushed are kept by inval.c.  This
2363 *	routine is a helper routine for inval.c.  Given a tuple belonging to
2364 *	the specified relation, find all catcaches it could be in, compute the
2365 *	correct hash value for each such catcache, and call the specified
2366 *	function to record the cache id and hash value in inval.c's lists.
2367 *	SysCacheInvalidate will be called later, if appropriate,
2368 *	using the recorded information.
2369 *
2370 *	For an insert or delete, tuple is the target tuple and newtuple is NULL.
2371 *	For an update, we are called just once, with tuple being the old tuple
2372 *	version and newtuple the new version.  We should make two list entries
2373 *	if the tuple's hash value changed, but only one if it didn't.
2374 *
2375 *	Note that it is irrelevant whether the given tuple is actually loaded
2376 *	into the catcache at the moment.  Even if it's not there now, it might
2377 *	be by the end of the command, or there might be a matching negative entry
2378 *	to flush --- or other backends' caches might have such entries --- so
2379 *	we have to make list entries to flush it later.
2380 *
2381 *	Also note that it's not an error if there are no catcaches for the
2382 *	specified relation.  inval.c doesn't know exactly which rels have
2383 *	catcaches --- it will call this routine for any tuple that's in a
2384 *	system relation.
2385 */
2386void
2387PrepareToInvalidateCacheTuple(Relation relation,
2388							  HeapTuple tuple,
2389							  HeapTuple newtuple,
2390							  void (*function) (int, uint32, Oid, void *),
2391							  void *context)
2392{
2393	slist_iter	iter;
2394	Oid			reloid;
2395
2396	CACHE_elog(DEBUG2, "PrepareToInvalidateCacheTuple: called");
2397
2398	/*
2399	 * sanity checks
2400	 */
2401	Assert(RelationIsValid(relation));
2402	Assert(HeapTupleIsValid(tuple));
2403	Assert(PointerIsValid(function));
2404	Assert(CacheHdr != NULL);
2405
2406	reloid = RelationGetRelid(relation);
2407
2408	/* ----------------
2409	 *	for each cache
2410	 *	   if the cache contains tuples from the specified relation
2411	 *		   compute the tuple's hash value(s) in this cache,
2412	 *		   and call the passed function to register the information.
2413	 * ----------------
2414	 */
2415
2416	slist_foreach(iter, &CacheHdr->ch_caches)
2417	{
2418		CatCache   *ccp = slist_container(CatCache, cc_next, iter.cur);
2419		uint32		hashvalue;
2420		Oid			dbid;
2421
2422		if (ccp->cc_reloid != reloid)
2423			continue;
2424
2425		/* Just in case cache hasn't finished initialization yet... */
2426		ConditionalCatalogCacheInitializeCache(ccp);
2427
2428		hashvalue = CatalogCacheComputeTupleHashValue(ccp, ccp->cc_nkeys, tuple);
2429		dbid = ccp->cc_relisshared ? (Oid) 0 : MyDatabaseId;
2430
2431		(*function) (ccp->id, hashvalue, dbid, context);
2432
2433		if (newtuple)
2434		{
2435			uint32		newhashvalue;
2436
2437			newhashvalue = CatalogCacheComputeTupleHashValue(ccp, ccp->cc_nkeys, newtuple);
2438
2439			if (newhashvalue != hashvalue)
2440				(*function) (ccp->id, newhashvalue, dbid, context);
2441		}
2442	}
2443}
2444
2445/* ResourceOwner callbacks */
2446
2447static void
2448ResOwnerReleaseCatCache(Datum res)
2449{
2450	ReleaseCatCacheWithOwner((HeapTuple) DatumGetPointer(res), NULL);
2451}
2452
2453static char *
2454ResOwnerPrintCatCache(Datum res)
2455{
2456	HeapTuple	tuple = (HeapTuple) DatumGetPointer(res);
2457	CatCTup    *ct = (CatCTup *) (((char *) tuple) -
2458								  offsetof(CatCTup, tuple));
2459
2460	/* Safety check to ensure we were handed a cache entry */
2461	Assert(ct->ct_magic == CT_MAGIC);
2462
2463	return psprintf("cache %s (%d), tuple %u/%u has count %d",
2464					ct->my_cache->cc_relname, ct->my_cache->id,
2465					ItemPointerGetBlockNumber(&(tuple->t_self)),
2466					ItemPointerGetOffsetNumber(&(tuple->t_self)),
2467					ct->refcount);
2468}
2469
2470static void
2471ResOwnerReleaseCatCacheList(Datum res)
2472{
2473	ReleaseCatCacheListWithOwner((CatCList *) DatumGetPointer(res), NULL);
2474}
2475
2476static char *
2477ResOwnerPrintCatCacheList(Datum res)
2478{
2479	CatCList   *list = (CatCList *) DatumGetPointer(res);
2480
2481	return psprintf("cache %s (%d), list %p has count %d",
2482					list->my_cache->cc_relname, list->my_cache->id,
2483					list, list->refcount);
2484}
